sections.paragraph:
ὄφελος, ους, τό (ὀφέλλω ‘increase’; Hom.+; OGI 519, 26 οὐδὲν ὄφελος ἡμεῖν; POxy 118 verso, 30 οὐδὲν ὄφ.; 1468, 6; Job 15:3; ApcSed 1, 3 [after 1 Cor 13:2]; Jos., Ant. 17, 154; Just.; Ath. 22, 8) an advantage derived from someth., benefit, good τί τὸ ὄφ.; what good does it do? Js 2:16 (Hierocles 14, 451; Philo, Migr. Abr. 55 and Just., D. 14, 1 τί γὰρ ὄφ.;). W. ἐάν foll. (cp. Ael. Aristid. 53 p. 640 D.; M. I. Brutus, Ep. 4) vs. 14; 2 Cl 6:2 (a saying of Jesus; in Mt 16:26 τί ὠφεληθήσεται ἄνθρωπος, ἐὰν … ;). τί μοι τὸ ὄφ.; what good is it to me? 1 Cor 15:32. τί μοι ὄφελος ταῦτα ἑωρακότι καὶ μὴ γινώσκοντι … ; how does it benefit me to have seen this and not to understand … ? Hv 3, 3, 1 (τί μοι ὄφελος; Chariton 7, 4, 10).—DELG s.v. 2 ὀφέλλω. M-M.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

ApcSed ApcSed = Apocalypse of Sedrach—List 2

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

someth. someth. = something

Hierocles Hierocles , Commentarius in aureum carmen, V a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

foll. foll. = followed, following

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Ael. Ael. Aristid. = Aelius Aristides, II a.d.—List 5

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle

2 Cl 2 Cl = 2 Clement—List 1

Hv Hv = Visions

Chariton Chariton , scriptor eroticus (writer of romances) I–II a.d.—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 743
